Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

CTN 4, 005 [Enuma Anu Enlil 20][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
o i 1'o 1'

[...] EN.NUN.AN.⸢USAN₂⸣ [...]1

(o i 1') [...] of the evening watch [...]

o i 2'2'

[...] ⸢šu⸣-ri-in-ni-šu ddele-bat ana ⸢ŠA₃⸣-[šu₂ ...]

(o i 2') [...] its šurīnu Venus [...] inside it: [...] of the king [...] to his father's throne; alternatively, to [...].

o i 3'3'

[...] LUGAL a-na gišGU.ZA AD-šu₂ KI.MIN a-na [...]

o i 4'4'

⸢AN⸣.GE₆ ša ina šu-ri-in-ni-šu ddele-bat ana ŠA₃-šu₂ KU₄ IGI-[ma]

(o i 4') You observe the eclipse in whose šurīnu Venus entered inside it and you observe its eclipsing and you take into account the west wind.

o i 5'5'

[KAN₅]-šu₂ IGI-ma tu₁₅MAR.TU ina ŠU-ka tu-kal

o i 6'6'

[...] A.GA.DE₃ki KI.MIN LUGAL A.GA.⸢DE₃⸣ki EŠ.BAR SUM

(o i 6') The decision is given [...] Akkad; alternatively, the king of Akkad. [...] of Akkad will die (but) his people will be healthy. [...] of Akkad will become confused (but) its future will be good.

o i 7'7'

[...] A.GA.DE₃ki UŠ₂ UN-MEŠ-šu₂ SILIM.MA

o i 8'8'

[...] A.GA.DE₃ki in-neš-ši EGIR-MEŠ-šu₂ SIG₅-MEŠ

o i 9'9'

[...] ⸢ša₂⸣ ina ⸢KAN₅⸣-šu₂ EN.NUN.U₄.ZAL.LI SAR-⸢ma⸣ 1/3-TA.AM₃

(o i 9') In Kislimu on the 28th day (or) the 29th day you observe the start of its appearance [...] that began the dawn watch in its eclipsing and delayed 1/3 of the watch and set in an eclipsed state.

o i 10'10'

[EN].⸢NUN uḫ⸣-ḫi-ru-ma ina ŠA₃.ḪUL ŠU₂u₂

o i 11'11'

ina ⸢itiGAN⸣ U₄ 28v-KAM U₄ 29-KAM SAG IGI.DU₈.A-šu₂ IGI-ma

o i 12'12'

ddele-bat ana ŠA₃-šu₂ KU₄ DUMU LUGAL ana E₂ AD-šu₂ ⸢KU₄⸣

(o i 12') (If) Venus enters inside it: the king's son will enter his father's house.

o i 13'13'

ina ⸢iti⸣GAN U₄ 28v-KAM SAG ⸢IGI⸣.DU₈.A-šu₂ IGI-ma

(o i 13') In Kislimu on the 28th day you observe the start of its appearance and you will (be able to) predict 1 hundred days of eclipse. The day of the moon's disappearance will show you the eclipse.

o i 14'14'

1 ME u₄-me AN.GE₆ DU₁₁.GA U₄.NA₂.A AN.GE₆ u₂-kal-lam-⸢ka⸣

o i 15'15'

* ina ⸢iti⸣BARA₂ U₄ 14-KAM AN.GE₆ GAR-ma DINGIR ina KAN₅-šu₂ A₂ tu₁₅SI*.SA₂*

(o i 15') (If) in Nisannu (I) on the 14th day an eclipse takes place and the god in its eclipsing becomes eclipsed on the north side above and clears on the north side below (and) sets in an eclipsed state. (There is a) west wind (and) it finishes its course in the evening watch:

o i 16'16'

⸢AN.TA⸣ KAN₅-ma A₂ tu₁₅SI.SA₂ KI.TA iz-ku ina ŠA₃.ḪUL ŠU₂

o i 17'17'

[tu₁₅]⸢MAR⸣.TU EN.NUN.AN.USAN₂ u₂-qat-ti DINGIR ša₂ ina KAN₅-šu₂

(o i 17') The god in whose eclipsing becomes eclipsed on the north [...] above and clears on the north side below [...] sets in an eclipsed state - you observe its eclipsing, and you take into account the west wind.

o i 18'18'

[...] tu₁₅SI.SA₂* AN.TA KAN₅-ma A₂ tu₁₅SI.SA₂ KI.TA iz-ku*-u₂

o i 19'19'

⸢ina*⸣ ŠA₃.ḪUL ŠU₂u₂ KAN₅-šu₂ IGI-ma tu₁₅MAR.TU ina ŠU-ka tu-kal

o i 20'20'

ina ŠA₃ A.GA.⸢DE₃⸣ki EŠ.BAR ⸢SUM⸣ LUGAL A.GA.DE₃ki UŠ₂

(o i 20') The decision is given for Akkad. The king of Akkad will die (but) his people will be healthy. The reign of Akkad will become confused (but) its future will be good.

o i 21'21'

UN-MEŠ-šu₂ SILIM.MA BAL A.GA.DE₃ki in-neš-[ši]

o i 22'22'

⸢EGIR⸣-MEŠ-šu₂ SIG₅-MEŠ ina itiBARA₂ U₄ 28v-KAM ina SAG IGI.DU₈.⸢A⸣-[šu₂ ...]

(o i 22') In Nisannu (I) on the 28th day [...] the start of its appearance [...].

o i 23'23'

⸢d⸣dele-bat ana ŠA₃-šu₂ KU₄ ⸢DUMU⸣ <LUGAL> ana E₂ ⸢AD⸣-šu₂ KU₄ ina iti[BARA₂ ...]

(o i 23') (If) Venus enters inside it: the king's son will enter his father's house.

(o i 23') In Nisannu (I) [...] you observe the start of its appearance and [...] 1 hundred days of eclipse [...]. The day of the moon's disappearance [...] eclipse. [...] ... confronting the king's son [...]

o i 24'24'

⸢SAG IGI.DU₈⸣.A-šu₂ IGI-ma 1 ME u₄-mi AN.GE₆ [...]

o i 25'25'

[... U₄.NA₂].⸢A⸣ AN.GE₆ [...]

o i 26'26'

[...] x-ri ma-ḫa-ri DUMU ⸢LUGAL⸣ [...]

o i 27'27'

[...] ⸢AN⸣.GE₆ GAR-ma DINGIR* ina [...]

(o i 27') [...] an eclipse takes place and the god in [...] is eclipsed on the [...] side [...].

o i 28'28'

[...] ⸢KAN₅⸣-ma ⸢A₂⸣ [...]

(traces of 1 line)
(rest of column missing)
Column ii
(start of column missing)
o ii 1'o 1'

[...] x [...]

(o ii 1') [...] ... you will (be able to) predict [...] of the eclipse. The day of the moon's disappearance will show you the eclipse.

o ii 2'2'

AN.GE₆ DU₁₁.GA [U₄.NA₂]-⸢AM₃⸣ AN.GE₆ u₂-[kal-lam-ka]

o ii 3'3'

* ina itiŠU U₄ 14-KAM AN.GE₆ GAR-ma* DINGIR ina KAN₅-šu₂ ⸢A₂⸣ [...]

(o ii 3') (If) in Duʾuzu on the 14th day an eclipse occurs and the god in its eclipsing becomes dark on the [...] side above and clears on the south side below (and there is) a north wind (and) in the evening watch it becomes halved and in its šurīnu the stars stand (and) come out above and below:

o ii 4'4'

AN.TA KAN₅-ma A₂ tu₁₅U₁₈.LU KI.TA iz-⸢ku⸣

o ii 5'5'

tu₁₅SI.SA₂ EN.NUN.AN.USAN₂ im-šul-ma ina šu-ri-in-[ni-šu₂]

o ii 6'6'

MUL-MEŠ e-liš u šap-liš : GUB-MEŠ E₃-MEŠ-[ni]

(o ii 6') The god that in its eclipsing becomes eclipsed on the east side above and clears on the north side below - you observe its eclipsing and you take into account the north wind.

o ii 7'7'

DINGIR ša₂!(U₄) ina KAN₅-šu₂ A₂ tu₁₅KUR.RA AN.TA ⸢KAN₅⸣-[ma]

o ii 8'8'

A₂ tu₁₅SI.SA₂ KI.TA iz-ku-u₂ KAN₅-šu₂ ⸢IGI⸣-[ma]

o ii 9'9'

tu₁₅SI.SA₂ ina ŠU-ka tu-kal ina ŠA₃ UNUGki [...]

(o ii 9') [...] for Uruk. In Uruk its festivals; alternatively, deaths [...]. The people will see abundance; alternatively, goodness will be good; alternatively, ... [...]. Business will prosper. A grandson, descendant of the king, in [...]. They will determine the reign. : If ... [...] he will die together with his family. In the evening watch [...].

o ii 10'10'

ina UNUGki EZEN-MEŠ-šu₂ KI.MIN UŠ₂-MEŠ [...]

o ii 11'11'

UN-⸢MEŠ⸣ ḪE₂.NUN IGI-MEŠ ⸢KI⸣.MIN SIG₅ DU₁₀ab KI.MIN! x [...]

o ii 12'12'

GANBA SIG₅-MEŠ <<ša₂>> DUMU DUMU lib₃-lib₃ LUGAL ina [...]

o ii 13'13'

BAL i-ša-am-mu : * x šum-ma na-ra-[...]2

o ii 14'14'

qa₂-du IM.⸢RI*⸣.A-šu₂ UŠ₂ EN.NUN.AN.⸢USAN₂⸣ [...]

o ii 15'15'

ana MUL GUR ina itiBARA₂ U₄ 28v-KAM ina SAG IGI.DU₈.A-[šu₂ ...]

(o ii 15') You return to the star. In Nisannu (I) on the 28th day at the start of its appearance [...] in Duʾuzu on the 14th day you will (be able to) predict the eclipse [...]. The day of the moon's disappearance will show you the eclipse.

o ii 16'16'

ina itiŠU U₄ 14-KAM AN.GE₆ ⸢DU₁₁⸣.[GA ...]

o ii 17'17'

U₄.⸢NA₂⸣-AM₃ AN.GE₆ u₂-kal-[lam-ka]

o ii 18'18'

* ina itiNE U₄ ⸢14-KAM AN.GE₆ GAR-ma DINGIR⸣ [...]

(o ii 18') (If) in Abu on the 14th day an eclipse occurs and the god [...] becomes eclipsed on the west side above and [...]; the north wind arises and in the middle watch [...].

o ii 19'19'

A₂ tu₁₅MAR.TU AN.TA ⸢KAN₅⸣-[ma ...]

o ii 20'20'

tu₁₅SI*.SA₂ ZI-ma EN.[NUN.MURU₂.BA ...]

o ii 21'21'

[ŠU].⸢NIR⸣ SI-šu₂ ša₂ ZA₃ tar-ṣa-⸢at ša₂⸣ [...]

(o ii 21') The šurīnu - its right horn is stretched out, its [...].

o ii 22'22'

[...] ina KAN₅-šu₂ ŠU.NIR SI-šu₂ ⸢ša₂⸣ [...]

(o ii 22') [...] in its eclipsing the šurīnu - its [...] horn [...] curved - you observe its eclipsing and [...] you [...] into account the north wind.

o ii 23'23'

[...] kap-pa-tu KAN₅-šu₂ IGI-⸢ma⸣ [...]

o ii 24'24'

[...] ⸢tu₁₅SI!.SA₂!⸣ ina ŠU-⸢ka⸣ [...]

o ii 25'25'

[... EŠ₃.NUN].⸢NA⸣ki [...]

(o ii 25') [...] Ešnuna [...]. [...] of his owner will kill his owner [...] because of the killing [...] he shall return to the city [...]. If his father's killer [...]. [...] his own to him [...]. If he returns to his beloved city [...].

o ii 26'26'

[... EN]-šu₂ EN-šu₂ ⸢i?⸣-[dak ...]

o ii 27'27'

[...] IGI GAZ ana IRI [...]

o ii 28'28'

[...] GUR BE GAZ AD-[šu₂ ...]

o ii 29'29'

[...] ⸢šu?⸣-a-tu₄ ana ša-⸢šu?⸣ [...]

Reverse
Column i
r i 1r i 1

BE <<x>> ana ⸢IRI*⸣ na*-ra*-mi*-šu₂* ⸢GUR*⸣ [...]

r i 22

ina iti⸢ŠU⸣ [ŠU].NIR na-bal-kut [...]

(r i 2) In Duʾuzu the šurīnu is crossed over [...]. [...] you will (be able to) predict an eclipse. Thus [...] its horns are bowed down and [...] the heavens. Starvation for Ešnuna ... [...].

r i 33

AN.GE₆ ta-⸢qab*⸣-bi ki-a-⸢am⸣ [...]

r i 44

SI-MEŠ-šu₂ kan-ša₂-ma ša₂-ma-[mi ...]

r i 55

ŠE.GAR* ana EŠ₃.NUN.NAki ina ⸢ŠA₃*⸣ U₄* 21*-⸢KAM*⸣ [...]

r i 66

* ina itiKIN U₄* 14-KAM ina SAG IGI-šu₂ IGI-ma [...]

(r i 6) (If) in Ululu on the 14th day at the start of its appearance you observe [...] it becomes eclipsed [...] above and in the east [...] (and there is a) west wind (and) [...] in the evening watch.

r i 77

AN.TA KAN₅-ma ina tu₁₅KUR*.RA [...]

r i 88

tu₁₅MAR.TU EN.NUN.AN.⸢USAN₂⸣ [...]

r i 99

ina KUR ur-ri ⸢ZI⸣-[ma ...]-KAM [...]

(r i 9) At the rising of the day [...] rises [...] and you observe the eclipsing [...] west wind.

r i 1010

u₃ KAN₅ IGI*-[ma ...] tu₁₅MAR.⸢TU⸣ [...]

r i 1111

ina ŠA₃ gu-⸢ti⸣-[umki ...] x x [...]

(r i 11) Concerning Gutium [...] ... [...] king [...] king [...].

r i 1212

⸢LUGAL⸣ [...] ⸢LUGAL⸣ [...]

r i 1313

ina iti⸢KIN U₄ 14!⸣-KAM SAG IGI-[šu₂ ...]

(r i 13) In Ululu, on the 14th day [...] the start of its appearance. (If) A star enters its left horn and stands (there): [...] A powerful man ... his property. The inhabitant of a hostile land [...] in a day.

r i 1414

MUL ⸢ana⸣ SI ZA₃-⸢šu₂⸣ KU₄-ma GUB x [...]

r i 1515

NIG₂.ŠU-⸢šu₂*⸣ KALA x DUMU KUR KUR₂ ina* ⸢U₄*⸣ [...]

r i 1616

tu₁₅SI.SA₂ ZI*-ma* GE₆ ig-mur-ma* [...]

(r i 16) The north wind arises and the night finishes and [...].

r i 1717

DINGIR* ša₂* ⸢ina⸣ KAN₅-šu₂ A₂ tu₁₅KUR.RA AN.[TA ...]

(r i 17) The god that in its eclipsing [...] on the east side above (and) clears on the south side below (and) in [...] - you observe the eclipsing and [...] into [...] the north wind.

r i 1818

A₂ tu₁₅⸢U₁₈⸣.LU KI.TA iz-ku-u₂ ina [...]

r i 1919

KAN₅ IGI-ma tu₁₅SI.SA₂ ina [...]

r i 2020

ina ŠA₃ LUGAL URIki EŠ*.BAR* SUM LUGAL URIki [...]

(r i 20) The decision is given for the king of Akkad. The king of Akkad [...] rebellion among them. A land will become embroiled in a small land for booty and a stable peace [...].

r i 2121

ina bi-ri-šu*-nu bar-tu₄-ma KUR ana KUR* TUR <ana> ši*-⸢la⸣-[a-ti]

r i 2222

i-ḫa-aq-ma sa-lim* ki-na-a-ti [...]

r i 2323

SILIM*.ME!(MAŠ) ki-na-a*-ti* ana* LUGAL IL₂ ina bi-[ri-šu-nu]

(r i 23) A stable peace will be brought to the king. Rebellion will take place among them and livestock will be destroyed.

r i 2424

bar-tu₄ GAR-ma MAŠ₂!(EREN₂.KUR)ANŠE* ZAḪ₂ ina itiNE* ⸢U₄* 28*⸣-[KAM]

(r i 24) In Abu on the 28th day you observe the start of its appearance and on the 14th day of Ululu you (will be able to) predict the eclipse. The day of the moon's disappearance will show you the eclipse.

r i 2525

SAG IGI.⸢DU₈⸣.A-šu₂ IGI-ma ina itiKIN U₄ 14-⸢KAM⸣ AN.⸢GE₆* ta*⸣-[qab-bi]

r i 2626

U₄.NA₂.A AN.GE₆ u₂-kal-lam-[ka]

r i 2727

* ina ⸢* itiDU₆ U₄ 14-KAM AN⸣.GE₆ [...] ⸢KAN₅⸣-šu₂ A₂ [...]

(r i 27) (If) in Tašritu on the 14th day an eclipse [...] in its eclipse on the [...] side.

(rest of column missing)
(rest of column missing)
Column ii
r ii 1r ii 1

[...] ⸢tu₁₅*⸣SI*.SA₂*

(r ii 1) [...] north [...] clears [...] halves (and) its right horn is bent up

r ii 22

[...] iz-ku*

r ii 33

[... im]-šul SI ZA₃-šu₂* kap*-⸢ṣa*⸣-at*

r ii 44

[...] x AN.TA KAN₅*-ma*

(r ii 4) [...] .. above is eclipsed [...] an eclipsed state (and) its right horn is bent up

r ii 55

[...] x ŠA₃!(U₂)ḪUL* SI* ZA₃*-šu₂* kap*-ṣa-at*

r ii 66

[... tu₁₅]MAR.TU ina ⸢ŠU-ka* tu-kal⸣

(r ii 6) [...] you take into account the west wind.

r ii 77

[...] SUM BAD₃.ANki SILIM-šu₂ qur-⸢ru⸣-ub

(r ii 7) [...] is given [...]. Der - its peace will be brought near. [...] ... [...] his land will not prosper. The king of the city will live long.

r ii 88

[...] x x [...] KUR-su NU SIG₅ LUGAL IRI ul-tab-bar

r ii 99

[...] BE AN ⸢NI*⸣ x [...] x ZA x EN*.NUN*.MURU₂* in-na-ṭal

(r ii 9) [...] ... [...] .... It becomes visible in the middle watch. In its eclipsing it goes until becoming halved. Thus is its omen and its decision.

r ii 1010

ina KAN₅*-šu₂ UGU ma-[ša]-⸢li₃⸣ DU ⸢ki⸣-a-⸢am GISKIM⸣-šu₂ EŠ.BAR-šu₂

r ii 1111

* ina itiAB U₄ 14-KAM AN.GE₆ GAR-ma DINGIR ina KAN₅-šu₂ A₂ tu₁₅KUR.RA

(r ii 11) (If) on the 14th day of Ṭebetu an eclipse takes place and the god in its eclipsing becomes eclipsed on the east side above and clears on the west side below, (there is a) west wind (and) it [...] complete the dawn watch.

r ii 1212

AN*.TA KAN₅-ma A₂ tu₁₅MAR*.TU* KI.TA iz-ku

r ii 1313

tu₁₅MAR.TU EN.NUN.U₄.ZAL.⸢LI⸣ [...] u₂-qat-⸢ti⸣

r ii 1414

SI-MEŠ-šu₂ mit-ḫa-ra <1>⸢et* la*⸣ ik-⸢bir 1et la⸣ iq-tin

(r ii 14) Its horns are symmetrical. One is not thick. One is not thin.

r ii 1515

DINGIR ša₂ ina KAN₅-šu₂ SI-⸢MEŠ⸣-šu₂ [... 1]et la ik-bi-ru

(r ii 15) The god in whose eclipsing its horns [...] -- one is not thick, one is not thin [...] -- you observe its eclipsing and you take into account the west wind.

r ii 1616

1et la! iq-ti-nu ⸢KAN₅⸣-šu₂ IGI-⸢ma⸣

r ii 1717

tu₁₅MAR.TU ina ŠU*-ka* tu*-⸢kal*⸣ ina ŠA₃ SU*.BIR₄*ki* EŠ*.BAR* SUM*

(r ii 17) The decision is given for Subartu. Subartu and Gutium will become hostile. Brother will smite brother. People will die. There will be many widows. The king of Subartu will make peace with the lands.

r ii 1818

⸢SU⸣.BIR₄ki* ⸢u* gu*⸣-ti*-⸢i*⸣ki* KUR₂?-MEŠ ŠEŠ ŠEŠ* i*-ra*-sib*

r ii 1919

UN*-MEŠ ŠUB-MEŠ ⸢al-ma⸣-na-a-tu [i]-min₃-da₃

r ii 2020

⸢LUGAL⸣ SU.BIR₄ki KUR.⸢KUR i⸣-sal-lim DINGIR ša₂ UGU ma-ša₂-⸢li⸣ [DU]ku

(r ii 20) The god who went until its becoming halved: thus is its omen (and) its decision.

r ii 2121

[ki]-a-am GISKIM-šu₂ EŠ.BAR-[šu₂]

r ii 2222

* ina itiZIZ₂ U₄ 14-KAM AN.GE₆ GAR-ma DINGIR ina KAN₅-šu₂ A₂ tu₁₅U₁₈.LU

(r ii 22) (If) on the 14th day of Šabaṭu an eclipse takes place and the god in its elcipsing becomes eclipsed on in the south side above and clears on the east side, above, (there is) a north wind (and) it begins the dawn watch and appears with the Sun.

r ii 2323

⸢AN⸣.TA KAN₅-ma A₂ tu₁₅⸢KUR.RA⸣ KI.TA iz-ku

r ii 2424

⸢tu₁₅⸣SI.SA₂ EN.NUN.U₄.ZAL.⸢LI SAR-ma⸣ KI ⸢dUTU⸣ <IGI> SI*-MEŠ-šu₂

(r ii 24) Its horns penetrate heaven. It [...] obscure all of its šurinnu and disappears.

r ii 2525

[AN]e nak-pa ŠU.NIR ka-⸢la-šu⸣ [x] ⸢DUL-ma⸣ it-bal

r ii 2626

⸢ina*⸣ U₄* ⸢28*⸣-KAM IGI-ma AN!(GIŠ)GE₆ ⸢qur*-ru*-ub⸣

(r ii 26) You observe on the 28th day and the eclipse is near. [...] it becomes red and it will show you the eclipse.

r ii 2727

[...] ⸢SA₅*-ma*⸣ AN.GE₆ u₂-kal-⸢lam⸣-ka DINGIR [...]

(r ii 27) The god [...] - you observe its eclipsing and you take into account the north wind.

r ii 2828

[...] KAN₅-šu₂ IGI-ma tu₁₅SI.SA₂ ina ŠU-ka tu-⸢kal⸣

r ii 2929

[... KA₂].DINGIR.RAki EŠ.⸢BAR⸣ [x KA₂].DINGIR.RAki ZAḪ₂-šu₂*

(r ii 29) The decision [...] Babylon. Babylon - its destruction [...] close together. Enlil [...] all of the land [...].

r ii 3030

[... qit]-ru-ub d!EN.[LIL₂] KUR KAL-ša₂

r ii 3131

[... an]-na*-a* ⸢iq⸣-bu-šu

(r ii 31) [...] to whom they said "yes" [...] his destruction is near. [...] ... [...]

r ii 3232

[...] ZAḪ₂-šu₂ qur-⸢ru⸣-[ub ...]

r ii 3333

[...] KI [...]

(rest of column missing)

1This tablet fills many gaps in Rochberg's edition of EAE Tablet 20 Recension A (ABCD, 1988, 179-212). While Wiseman's copy of the reverse was available to Rochberg, it is often defective, and she had no access to the obverse (copied later by Black). This reading of the tablet has benefited enormously from collation (marked *), although some passages remain unclear and would profit from closer study.

2The sign before šumma is probably an erasure (collated) ## look at this line again with photos

Created by Eleanor Robson for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2009 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P363420
